arts.ttl: Arsenal 'make approach' for £17.5m midfielder compared to Jude Bellingham, Steven Gerrard
arts.sum: Arsenal supposedly make an approach to sign Juventus midfielder Khephren Thuram, who has been likened to Jude Bellingham and Steven Gerrard.

arts.ttl: Exclusive: What Aston Villa sources are saying about Boubacar Kamara future as Arsenal and other PL giants circle
arts.sum: TBR Football can exclusively confirm that sources at Aston Villa are saying about Boubacar Kamara’s future amid interest from the likes of Arsenal, Liverpool and...

arts.ttl: Gabriel Arsenal replacement named as Arteta set for crucial Everton decision
arts.sum: With Arsenal travelling to Everton on Saturday, Arteta is now looking for a new centre-back to partner William Saliba at the heart of defence. The Poland international is a natural replacement in the heart of defence, and with injury concerns surrounding White, Calafiori and Timber, Arteta can afford few risks in the biggest game of the season.

arts.ttl: Everton vs. Arsenal: What Mikel Arteta's Gunners must do to avoid 112-year worst at Goodison Park
arts.sum: Arsenal have been told what they must do in Saturday's Premier League showdown with Everton to avoid suffering an unwanted 112-year first at Goodison Park.

arts.ttl: Arsenal: Mikel Arteta must address biggest blind spot against Everton with one eye on Real Madrid
arts.sum: Squad management is often pointed to as Mikel Arteta’s biggest weakness

arts.ttl: Kylian Mbappe and Antonio Rudiger punished by UEFA but cleared for Arsenal game
arts.sum: Real Madrid can breathe a sigh of relief after their players will not face a ban from UEFA which would have ruled them out of the first leg of Champions League semi-final against Arsenal. It was announced last week that Dani Ceballos, Vinicius Junior, Kylian Mbappe and Antonio Rudiger were under investigation for their conduct […] The post Kylian Mbappe and Antonio Rudiger punished by UEFA but cleared for Arsenal game appeared first on Football España.

With only four matches left at Goodison, Evertonians will be desperate to extend their three-game unbeaten run on their own patch right until the end, but we’ll be looking to make our final trip memorable by clinching back-to-back wins at the famous old ground for the first time in 13 years, and build momentum with Real Madrid on the horizon. Back in January, Sean Dyche’s two-year stint as Everton boss ended with his side sitting just a point above the relegation zone after winning just one of their previous 11 games.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid players fined but free to face Arsenal
arts.sum: Four Real Madrid players, including Kylian Mbappe and Vinicius Jr, are cleared to face Arsenal in their Champions League quarter-final despite three being punished by Uefa.

arts.ttl: Arsenal star sent brutal transfer response as club set for major financial loss
arts.sum: Former Tottenham striker Darren Bent has suggested Arsenal ought to consider selling Gabriel Jesus in the summer transfer window to raise funds for new attacking signings. Despite having two years remaining on his contract, Jesus' future at the Emirates looks in doubt after a lacklustre season, contributing with seven goals and two assists in 27 matches.

arts.ttl: Report: What Andrea Berta privately thinks about signing Matheus Cunha and making him Arsenal's new striker
arts.sum: Cunha ahead of Isak, Sesko and Gyokeres?
